_doc: What CIVVIS does with each shipped Civ VI modifier EffectType.
A status describes whether CIVVIS' ENGINE can express the effect for the
content it models -- not whether every row's owning object exists here.
Content scope is measured separately, by the 'Only in Civ VI' columns of
tools/civ6_fidelity.py. An effect is 'implemented' only when a concrete
execution path exists; when in doubt the honest status is 'partial'.
These are inspection judgements, seeded by reading the engine for each
effect family. They are not yet verified row by row against the game.
Per-row verification is what turns this census into a proof, and it is the
next step; until then treat 'implemented' as a claim to be checked, not a
guarantee. Anything absent from this file counts as unmodelled, so newly
shipped content raises the backlog rather than hiding in it.

A 'verified' entry has had every shipped row drilled with --effect and matched against the CIVVIS path that covers it, or attributed to content CIVVIS does not model. Everything else is still an inspection judgement.

ATTACH_MODIFIER.note: The interpreter primitive: attach another modifier to a collection. CIVVIS hardcodes effects per object, so it has no general way to express this. Implementing it is phase 2 itself, and it gates the long tail behind it.

ATTACH_MODIFIER_TO_PLAYERTYPE.note: World Congress outcomes attaching modifiers to a class of players. Congress resolutions are hardcoded per resolution instead.

DIPLOMACY_SIMPLE_EFFECT.note: Bespoke leader agendas, which MECHANICS.md scopes out deliberately. Almost every row is a TraitModifier on a leader CIVVIS does not model.

ADJUST_PLAYER_STRENGTH_MODIFIER.note: Verified row by row. All 48 promotion Combat Strength amounts CIVVIS models match the shipped values exactly, under CIVVIS' own semantic effect names. The Giant Death Robot's four tech upgrades were attached to the wrong nodes: Advanced Power Cells ships the Particle Beam Siege Cannon and Cybernetics Enhanced Mobility, while CIVVIS had the movement on Advanced Power Cells and invented a healing upgrade on Cybernetics that no shipped row grants. Now corrected. The remaining rows are unit abilities for leaders and city-states outside CIVVIS' roster.

ADJUST_UNIT_MOVEMENT.note: Movement grants execute from promotions, support units and tree_effects (embarked_movement, adjacent_movement).

ADJUST_UNIT_EXPERIENCE_MODIFIER.note: XP scaling from promotions, Oligarchy and city-state bonuses.

ADJUST_UNIT_GRANT_EXPERIENCE.note: Flat XP grants on training and from Great People.

ADJUST_UNIT_HEALING_MODIFIERS.note: Medic, Supply Convoy and promotion healing all execute.

ADJUST_UNIT_SPY_OPERATION_CHANCE.note: The espionage promotion tree adjusts operation odds.

ENABLE_UNIT_FAITH_PURCHASE.note: Verified row by row. Theocracy and the Grand Master's Chapel enable Faith purchase for exactly ten land combat classes -- melee, ranged, anti-cavalry, light/heavy cavalry, light/heavy chariot, ranged cavalry, recon and siege. CIVVIS admitted every land military unit, which also let Theocracy Faith-buy a Giant Death Robot; the GDR is its own class outside the list, and is now excluded. The naval and Archaeologist rows are leader traits, and the Spy row is Wu Zetian -- content CIVVIS does not model.

ADJUST_TRADE_ROUTE_CAPACITY.note: Verified row by row, and it found two gaps now fixed. A city earns up to two routes from its own infrastructure and each is an either/or: the Commercial Hub grants one and a Harbor grants it only where there is no Commercial Hub (REQUIRES_NO_COMMERCIAL_HUB), with England's Royal Navy Dockyard unconditional; separately a Market grants one and a Lighthouse only where there is no Market (REQUIRES_NO_MARKET). CIVVIS had only the building tier. Merchant Republic's two routes were also missing. Foreign Trade, Colossus, Great Zimbabwe, Carthage and the Great Merchants already executed. Alliance-level routes remain unmodelled.

ADJUST_GREAT_PERSON_POINTS.note: Verified row by row, and it found three wrong policy cards. Laissez-Faire is +4 Great Merchant empire-wide plus 2 per Bank, 4 per Stock Exchange, 4 Great Admiral per Seaport and 2 per Shipyard; Nobel Prize is +4 Great Scientist plus 2 per University, 4 per Research Lab, 2 Great Engineer per Factory and 4 per Power Plant; Military Organization was missing its flat +4 Great General. CIVVIS had collapsed the first two into one flat number per building tier. Strategos, Inspiration, Revelation, Literary Tradition, Navigation, Traveling Merchants, Frescoes, Invention and Symphonies were already exact. Divine Spark and the Commemoration/Zhangye Danxia rows are pantheon and content scope.

ADJUST_CITY_TOURISM.note: City Tourism from governors, policies and buildings.

ADJUST_DISTRICT_TOURISM_CHANGE.note: Per-district Tourism from buildings and civics.

ADJUST_CITY_IDENTITY_PER_TURN.note: Loyalty per turn from buildings, policies, governors and Great People.

ADJUST_CITY_FREE_POWER.note: Verified row by row: Geothermal Plant 4, Hydroelectric Dam 6, Wind/Solar/Offshore Wind 2 each, Reyna's Renewable Subsidizer +2 on each of those five, and Aerospace Contractors' Spaceport 3 all execute with the shipped amounts. The three remaining rows are Cardiff, a city-state outside CIVVIS' modelled roster, which is content scope rather than effect scope.

ADJUST_CITY_GREATWORK_YIELD.note: Great Work yields, slots and Culture/Tourism output execute.

ADJUST_PLAYER_GOVERNOR_POINTS.note: Governor titles from civics, Government Plaza buildings and Great People.

ADJUST_DISTRICT_YIELD_MODIFIER.note: Percentage district yield modifiers from policies and governors.

TERRAIN_ADJACENCY.note: District adjacency is audited source by source at zero divergence.

ADD_RELIGIOUS_BELIEF_YIELD.note: Follower and founder belief yields; the Beliefs table audits clean.

ADJUST_BUILDING_PRODUCTION.note: Production toward buildings from policies, governors and Great People (item_prod_mult).

ADJUST_UNIT_TAG_ERA_PRODUCTION.note: Verified row by row. The amounts and class groupings were already right, but the effect's whole point is the era window and CIVVIS ignored it: Agoge boosted a Modern Infantry as readily as a Warrior, and every one of the nine cards stacked across all nine eras. Each card now carries its shipped window -- Agoge 0-1, Feudal Contract 2-3, Grande Armee 4-5, Military First 6-7, Chivalry 0-4, Lightning Warfare 5-7, Defense of the Motherland 5-7, Strategic Air Force 6-7, International Waters 0-7 -- checked against the era of the node that unlocks the unit. Letters of Marque, Patriotic War and Finest Hour are cards CIVVIS does not carry.

ADJUST_CITY_YIELD_MODIFIER.note: Verified row by row for the wonders, policies and governments CIVVIS models. Amundsen-Scott, Broadway, Casa de Contratacion and Kilwa Kisiwani carry their shipped percentages; Colonial Taxes was missing the +10% Production that accompanies its +25% Gold on foreign continents, now applied. Communism and Corporate Libertarianism yields, Disinformation Campaign and the Great Work rows remain content or systems CIVVIS scopes differently; POLICY_INQUISITION belongs to a game mode outside the baseline.

GRANT_UNIT_IN_CITY.note: Verified row by row against the wonders, beliefs and buildings CIVVIS models. Colossus, Pyramids, Statue of Zeus, Kotoku-in, Mahabodhi Temple, Meenakshi Temple, Fertility Rites, Religious Settlements and the tribal-village grants all execute with the shipped counts. The Statue of Liberty's two free Settlers were missing and are now granted. Remaining rows are leader traits, Government Plaza Spy/Builder grants and a Commemoration Giant Death Robot -- content CIVVIS does not model.

GRANT_YIELD.note: Instant yields from Great People activation and goody huts.

ADJUST_BUILDING_YIELD_CHANGE.note: Verified row by row. Third Alternative, Military Research, the Electronics Factory Culture, the Tsikhe Faith, Choral Music, Feed the World and Isaac Newton's University Science all match. The city-state Envoy building bonuses did not: the shipped rows pay 2 for the first two building tiers whatever the type, 3 for the third (6 for Trade), and 2/4 Consulate and 3/6 Chancery. CIVVIS paid 1 for the Capital and the first tier and doubled the second tier for Trade. Corrected. Still partial for the structural reason: an arbitrary modifier cannot adjust an arbitrary building's yield, only the named sources here.
leads: CIVVIS grants Trade Envoy bonuses to the Shipyard and Stock Exchange; the shipped rows name only Market, Lighthouse, Bank and Seaport.

ADJUST_PLOT_YIELD.note: Improvement tech and civic upgrades execute through tree_effects, and named wonder and belief plot effects execute; arbitrary plot-yield grants do not.

GRANT_ABILITY.note: Promotions and a fixed set of named unit abilities execute, but the general UnitAbilities system that most of these rows target does not exist. Most rows here are Great Person birth abilities.

CITY_GRANT_RANDOM_RESOURCE_PRODUCT.note: Products and Corporations exist and are capped and movable, but the project-completion random grant is not wired.
